title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
summary: Jack Roberts discusses Claude Fable 5 being unexpectedly removed and recommends running AI models locally using Ollama to maintain access independent of cloud provider actions. He demonstrates building a local NotebookLM alternative with open notebook and sets up model routing with OpenRouter across Claude, DeepSeek, and other providers.
writeup: The video responds to Claude Fable 5 being unexpectedly removed, arguing that local model hosting reduces dependence on cloud provider decisions. Jack Roberts demonstrates running models locally with Ollama and building a local NotebookLM alternative using open notebook. He also configures model routing through OpenRouter across Claude, DeepSeek, and other providers. The takeaway is a setup combining local inference and multi-provider routing to preserve access when a hosted model disappears.
